intro.name: Patrick
intro.description: I'm 17 years old and currently in my third year of apprenticeship as an Application Developer EFZ. I started my apprenticeship at Swisscom (Switzerland) Ltd. in summer 2023.

about.title: My
about.paragraph1: I am a motivated developer with experience in creating user-friendly web applications. My journey began with HTML and CSS and led me to mastering modern frameworks like {vue} and {express}.
about.paragraph2: In my first programming project at Swisscom, I had the opportunity to learn basic skills such as HTML, CSS and JavaScript. Later in the project, I got my first insight into the Angular framework.
about.paragraph3: In later projects, I worked with Vue.js and Express.js. I also had the opportunity to mentor other apprentices and share my knowledge with them in the role of a project provider.

list.title: FirstSteps
list.longDescription: I started my apprenticeship in the First Steps. There I learned everything about Swisscom and set up my work devices with various tools and also learned how to use them. We also looked at topics such as occupational safety. They served to prepare us for the apprenticeship.

list.title: Team HIVE
list.longDescription: In Team HIVE I had my introduction to programming. I learned the basics of HTML, CSS, JS, TS, Angular and GIT. I also learned to work in an agile manner and became familiar with the Jira tool. We were able to implement these technologies in many different practical exercises. In the project, we had the freedom to work at our own pace and acquire a good basic knowledge for the apprenticeship.

list.title: Minds in the Cloud
list.description: Leading a group of apprentices in the field of Cloud & IT
list.longDescription: My colleagues Janic Moos and Panat Ruangsri, together with me, led the Minds in the Clouds group (a group of apprentices in the field of Cloud & IT). We met once a month, when possible, to exchange ideas and discuss various topics. It also served us to make new contacts, which is very important in professional life. My colleagues and I led and planned the team meetings and organized tours when the group agreed. This resulted, for example, in a tour of the Swisscom data center in Wankdorf. This was a side project.

list.title: Core Automation Team (CAT)
list.description: In this team I got to know the technologies Vue.js and Nuxt.js
list.longDescription: I was welcomed into the agile Core Automation Team (CAT) in February 2024. In this team, I got to know the technologies Vue.js and Nuxt.js. I was able to work on a website from the very beginning, which shows all the automations of our team. Until the end, I was able to work on this page until the final touches. In this team, we worked according to the agile sAFE.

list.title: Application Developer@NexCC
list.description: At NexCC I was able to develop applications using Vue.js, Express.js, PowerApps and PowerAutomate.
list.longDescription: At the Next Competence Center, I had the opportunity to acquire many technologies. I started with an introductory task with Vue.js in the frontend and Express.js in the backend. Later I implemented an application for the first time with a low-code program called PowerApps. I implemented permission management and automation with PowerAutomate. In the project, I also had the opportunity to get a taste of support.

list.title: PA@NexCC
list.description: I had the opportunity to do a second project at NexCC as a project provider.
list.longDescription: After my project "Application Developer@NexCC" I had the opportunity to complete another project at the Next Competence Center as a project provider. I had to take on a lot of responsibility, as my original project provider was absent for a longer period of time and I was allowed to supervise two apprentices instead of one. I gave the apprentices Colin Walker and Nico Benninger an introduction to Vue.js at the beginning. They were able to apply this in their introductory task and shortly afterwards also in the Project Archive. We implemented this with Vue.js, Nest.js and a Docker pipeline within 6 days. I then also gave them an introduction to the Power Platforms PowerApps, PowerAutomate and Sharepoint. They also had the opportunity to gain insights into support. While the apprentices were working diligently, I also implemented some tools in PowerApps and worked on the Project Archive.

list.title: Team Halo
list.description: In this project, I acquired knowledge in Java and in the development of MCP.
list.longDescription: In the project, I started with a starter app to learn the required technologies (Springboot, AWS, MCP and more). Later we worked on productive orders to simplify internal processes with AI.

list.title: AI-Challange@Ost
list.description: Nebenprojekt an der Fachhochschule Ostschweiz, bei dem ich mit meinem Dreierteam selbstfahrende Autos mit KI programmierte. Wir entwickelten ein Fahrmodell, das auf Schilder reagiert und Linien bei verschiedenen Lichtverhältnissen folgt, und holten den ersten Platz.
list.longDescription: An der AI-Challenge@OST habe ich als externes Nebenprojekt an der Fachhochschule Ostschweiz teilgenommen und zusammen mit meinem Dreierteam ein selbstfahrendes Fahrzeug von Grund auf programmiert. Die Challenge erstreckte sich über drei Events am Campus Rapperswil-Jona zwischen Oktober und Dezember 2025. Wir starteten am 25. Oktober mit einer Einführung in die AI-Programmierung. Zwischen den Events arbeiteten wir remote an verschiedenen Aufgaben, die bereits für die finale Challenge bewertet wurden. Unser Coach unterstützte uns dabei kontinuierlich. Unser Fahrmodell musste komplexe Herausforderungen meistern: Das Auto reagierte autonom auf Verkehrsschilder, folgte präzise einer Linie und bewältigte verschiedene Lichtverhältnisse sowie wechselnde Hintergründe. Wir investierten zwischen den Events ca. 5-8 Stunden pro Woche in die Entwicklung und Optimierung. Am 6. Dezember traten wir gegen alle anderen Teams an und holten den ersten Platz. Dafür gewannen wir Digitec Gutscheine im Wert von CHF 1000 pro Person. Das Event bot mir nicht nur technisches Know-How im Bereich AI und autonomes Fahren, sondern auch wertvolle Erfahrungen in Teamarbeit und Problemlösung unter Zeitdruck. Für mehr Detail: AI-Challange@Ost

list.title: [M]INI NET
list.longDescription:
Ich bin bei [M]INI NET die erste Ansprechperson und erfasse Aufträge in unserem Auftragspool. Dabei repräsentiere ich [M]INI NET professionell gegenüber unseren Kunden. Das Team unterstützt mich dabei, meine Komfortzone zu verlassen und auch mal bereichsübergreifend zu arbeiten.

Zusätzlich zu unserem bestehenden Media- und Admin-Team kann ich von Anfang an beim Aufbau eines neuen KI- und Automatisierungsteams dabei sein! In diesem Team kann ich die Zukunft aktiv mitgestalten und Verantwortung übernehmen.

KI und Automatisierung sind zwei der spannendsten Themen unserer Zeit. Auch wir bei [M]INI NET wollen diese Entwicklungen aktiv nutzen und vorantreiben. Dabei lerne ich, moderne Tools produktiv einzusetzen und mein Wissen direkt in die Organisation einzubringen.

In diesem Pilotprojekt bilde ich mit anderen ein starkes Team, das Innovation, Kreativität und technisches Verständnis vereint. Gemeinsam gestalten wir Neues, probieren aus und setzen eigene Ideen um – unterstützt von [M]INI NET und erfahrenen Fachcoaches.

list.title: AI-Challenge@OST - 1st Place
list.description: Winner of the AI Challenge at the University of Applied Sciences Eastern Switzerland with self-driving car
list.longDescription:
I participated in the AI-Challenge@OST as an external side project at the University of Applied Sciences Eastern Switzerland and together with my team of three programmed a self-driving vehicle from scratch. The challenge spanned three events at the Rapperswil-Jona campus between October and December 2025.

We started on October 25 with an introduction to AI programming. Between events, we worked remotely on various tasks that were already evaluated for the final challenge. Our coach supported us continuously.

Our driving model had to master complex challenges: The car reacted autonomously to traffic signs, followed a line precisely, and handled different lighting conditions and changing backgrounds. We invested about 5-8 hours per week between events in development and optimization.

On December 6, we competed against all other teams and won first place. For this, we won Digitec vouchers worth CHF 1000 per person.
